message.content:
Resource Hijacking involves adversaries leveraging compromised systems to solve resource-intensive problems, often for cryptocurrency mining, which can degrade system performance and availability. This technique is commonly used to validate cryptocurrency transactions and earn virtual currency. Adversaries may target servers, cloud-based systems, user endpoints, and containerized environments due to their resource availability and scalability. They may also eliminate competing malware to monopolize resources. Additionally, hijacked systems can be used in botnets for Network Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks or to distribute malicious torrents.

Common adversary tactics include deploying cryptocurrency mining tools like XMRIG, Monero miners, and other specialized malware. Notable groups such as APT41, Blue Mockingbird, and TeamTNT have been observed using these methods.

Mitigation strategies include monitoring and limiting resource usage, securing APIs, implementing robust endpoint protection, and regularly updating and patching systems. Network segmentation and anomaly detection can also help identify and isolate compromised systems.
